articles.title: House breaks ranks on Haiti TPS, forcing Trump to face a bipartisan rebellion
articles.summary: A discharge petition led by Rep. Ayanna Pressley has pushed Haiti Temporary Protected Status back to the House floor, exposing tensions inside the GOP and setting up a politically charged vote.

articles.title: IMF says Iran war has jolted the world economy, lifting inflation and dimming growth
articles.summary: The Fund’s April outlook warns that energy shocks and war risk could keep prices elevated and squeeze already fragile global growth.

articles.title: NIH-backed CRISPR advance could make gene editing smaller, safer and easier to deliver
articles.summary: A new NIH-funded gene-editing system trims down one of medicine’s biggest bottlenecks: getting CRISPR into the right cells without losing precision.

articles.title: At 50, 'Jaws' is back in the spotlight as Universal readies a new theatrical reissue
articles.summary: The summer-movie classic is returning to theaters this year, reviving a half-century conversation about the blockbuster era, theatrical exhibition and why old movies still draw crowds.

articles.title: OpenAI and Google push enterprise AI into the workflow as the market shifts from demos to deployment
articles.summary: Fresh announcements from OpenAI, Google and Anthropic show the AI race moving beyond model launches toward adoption, integration and enterprise infrastructure.

articles.title: TSMC’s record quarter underscores how AI has become the chip industry’s main engine
articles.summary: The world’s largest contract chipmaker posted stronger-than-expected quarterly results and warned that geopolitical shocks could still jolt the AI hardware boom.

articles.title: Artemis II’s moon flyby is over, but its most surprising impact may be on what comes next
articles.summary: NASA’s first crewed lunar flight in more than 50 years delivered the expected spectacle — and a new burst of scientific and political pressure around the Moon’s next chapter.

articles.title: Philippines deepens South China Sea push with new base and allied drills
articles.summary: Manila is hardening its maritime posture in the disputed South China Sea as it opens a new coast guard command on Thitu Island and prepares for expanded U.S.-led exercises with Japan and other partners.

articles.title: Coachella 2026 turns into a headline-making pop culture test for Sabrina Carpenter, Justin Bieber and Karol G
articles.summary: With weekend one underway and weekend two set for April 17-19, Coachella’s 2026 lineup has become a live referendum on pop stardom, nostalgia and the festival’s still-mighty cultural pull.

articles.title: Sudan enters fourth year of war with aid workers warning the world has moved on
articles.summary: At a Berlin donor conference, officials warned that Sudan’s war is deepening while global attention shifts elsewhere, leaving millions displaced, hungry and at risk.
